headline: Malaysia-Indonesia Maritime Boundary Unresolved 15 Years
context: Malaysia and Indonesia failed to finalize maritime boundaries in Celebes Sea and Malacca Strait after 15 years of talks.

cards.big: Malaysia-Indonesia maritime boundary talks stalled for 15 years
cards.sub: Unresolved boundaries affect fishing and energy rights in both areas

cards.big: Overlapping claims cover approximately 28,000 square kilometres
cards.sub: Area contains estimated 800 million barrels of oil equivalent

cards.big: 480 Malaysian fishermen detained by Indonesian authorities since 2020
cards.sub: Fishermen unknowingly cross disputed lines losing boats and equipment

cards.big: Joint development zone proposed in 2023 but Indonesia demanded 60-40 split
cards.sub: Malaysia insists on 50-50 consistent with UNCLOS equidistance principle

cards.big: Boundary disputes test whether bilateral negotiations can resolve overlapping maritime claims
15 years without resolving the Indonesia maritime boundary. Undrawn lines create space for resource disputes.
